steps.title: Identify the key constraint
steps.content: The passage emphasizes that the people show no evidence of having traveled extensively outside Siberia. This is the critical limiting factor that determines how Near Eastern dogs and artifacts could have appeared there.

steps.title: Determine how items reached Siberia
steps.content: The evidence shows Near Eastern dogs interbred with Siberian dogs and Near Eastern artifacts appeared in Siberia. Since the Siberians did not travel to the Near East, they couldn't have brought these items back themselves—they must have been transported to Siberia. Choice A states exactly this: dogs and artifacts transported from the Near East to Siberia. Choice B contradicts the passage by claiming Siberians traveled to the Near East, while choices C and D both reverse the documented direction of what moved. That matches choice A — A is correct.
